metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1039
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This publication provides evidence that liprin beta has a role in synapse formation in drosophila. The authors use two liprin beta null lines (B-delta45 and B-delta83) at tthe fly NMJ.

Liprin beta null flies displayed a defect in targeting of axons in the muscle. 14-17% of R7 axons showed projections extending beyond its target (Figure 4). In addition, beta null had a reduction in the number of NMJ boutons (Figure 6). The latter phenotype could be rescued by expression of liprin beta in the nerve or in the muscle. Thus, liprin beta can act both pre- and postsynaptically.

It is not known whether liprin beta has a direct role in synapse formation, the synapse formation might be a secondary effect of the axonal targeting defect.

6/6/2018 Pim
- I have chosen term 'regulation of synapse assembly (GO:0051963)'. Another annotation to term 'synaptic target recognition (GO:0008039)' might have been possible.. Experimental description: We show here that the single Drosophila Liprin-beta and the Drosophila ortholog of KazrinE, which we have named Liprin-gamma, both physically interact with Liprin-alpha.
